8A_complex_gap_pairs.finding: Transform invariance is absolute for all tested encodings. Interleaved (n=196) and magnitude (n=98) both produce pattern-invariant transform values. CAILculator's bilateral kernel is invariant to pattern_id for any 1D real-valued input, regardless of encoding strategy or array length.
8A_complex_gap_pairs.color_group_clustering: N/A — invariance confirmed, Color Group structure requires multidimensional native MCP support
8A_complex_gap_pairs.phase9_design_implication: To activate Color Group sensitivity, CAILculator must be extended to accept native vector-valued or complex inputs at the kernel level, not via scalar encoding tricks.

gue_theoretical.note: Symmetry decreased from 7A (96.7%) — finer grid resolves sinc² asymmetry more sharply

empirical_zeros.note: Level repulsion clearly visible at alpha=0.1 (R=0.02) and alpha=0.2 (R=0.09)

8B_fine_grid_pair_correlation.new_record_note: 12.5 pts crushes Phase 5 record of 7.2 pts — fine-grid R(alpha) is substantially more discriminating than spacing ratios
8B_fine_grid_pair_correlation.outcome_classification: Separation > 7.2 pts — fine-grid R(alpha) sets new record

8C_extended_height.finding: Zeros 500-1000 (n=499) show actual symmetry = GUE seed 1 exactly (72.4%), and GUE mean of 75.0%. Delta = -2.6 pts. The Phase 5B positive delta (+1.5) was window-specific; the full upper-half block gives a slightly negative delta, consistent with the Phase 7B and 8A negative delta direction. Both directions are within sampling variance for n=499.

key_findings.8A: Transform invariance is absolute and encoding-independent. All 6 Canonical Six patterns produce identical transform values for any 1D real-valued array. This is now a confirmed structural theorem of the CAILculator bilateral kernel. Pattern differentiation requires native vector/complex input support at the kernel level.
key_findings.8B: NEW RECORD — 12.5 pt GUE/Poisson separation via fine-grid R(alpha). Doubles the Phase 5 record of 7.2 pts. The fine grid (n=150, Δα=0.1) resolves the sinc² oscillatory structure much more finely than the coarse grid (n=30, Δα=0.5). Level repulsion is directly readable: empirical R(0.1)=0.02 vs Poisson=1.0. This is the strongest Chavez discrimination result in the entire RH series.
key_findings.8C: Zeros 500-1000 (n=499) confirm GUE-consistent symmetry at 72.4%, matching GUE seed 1 exactly. GUE mean across 3 seeds = 75.0%, giving delta = -2.6 pts. The Phase 5B anomalous +1.5 result was a narrow-window effect. The negative delta direction (actual < GUE synthetic) is consistent across Phase 7B, 8A, and 8C, suggesting a systematic pattern.
key_findings.combined: Phase 8 delivers one headline result (8B: 12.5 pt record), confirms the invariance theorem (8A), and resolves the Phase 5B anomaly (8C). The dominant next direction is Phase 9: achieve the first result using the FINE-GRID R(alpha) setup with higher-resolution zeros (Odlyzko dataset or zeros 1000+) to push separation further. Secondary: implement native vector input in CAILculator to test Color Group clustering.

cumulative_records.pattern_differentiation: 0 pts — transform invariance confirmed absolute through Phase 8

actual_vs_gue_best.overall_direction: Negative delta consistent across Phases 7B, 8A, 8C (actual < GUE synthetic)
